description:
Create a new MCP tool `check_shadow_dom_usage` that analyzes consumer CSS/HTML to detect Shadow DOM styling anti-patterns.

**What it checks:**
1. CSS selectors that try to pierce Shadow DOM (descendant selectors targeting known internal class names)
2. `!important` usage that won't work across shadow boundaries
3. Missing or incorrect CSS custom property names (fuzzy match against component's documented properties)
4. Incorrect `::part()` usage (parts that don't exist, chained parts, descendant selectors after ::part)
5. JavaScript shadow root penetration (`shadowRoot.querySelector` style manipulation)
6. `::slotted()` misuse in consumer code (::slotted is only valid inside Shadow DOM)

**Implementation:**
- New handler: `src/handlers/shadow-dom-checker.ts`
- New test: `tests/handlers/shadow-dom-checker.test.ts` (TDD)
- New tool registration in a new `src/tools/styling.ts` tool group
- Takes: CSS content string or file path + component library CEM context
- Returns: Array of diagnostics with severity (error/warning/info), line numbers, suggested fixes

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Detects at least the 6 anti-patterns listed above
- Each diagnostic includes: type, severity, message, suggestion (correct approach)
- Tests cover each anti-pattern type with real-world examples from wc-libraries analysis
- Must pass lint, format, type-check
title: New MCP tool: check_shadow_dom_usage — anti-pattern detector for consumer code
